name: Taipei Open
stakes: This week is about building on PV Sindhu's recent resurgence and providing a platform for India's other top shuttlers to gain crucial momentum and ranking points at a Super 300 event.
summary:
The Indian contingent arrives at the Taipei Open, a Super 300 event, with renewed optimism, particularly surrounding PV Sindhu. Fresh off a spectacular Japan Open triumph where she defeated top contenders like Akane Yamaguchi and Chen Yufei, Sindhu has emphatically answered her doubters. Her coach, Pullela Gopichand, has lauded her new-found surge, suggesting she's a threat to top players once more. This tournament offers a crucial opportunity for Sindhu to build on that momentum and consolidate her form.

While the draw for the Taipei Open is yet to be announced, other Indian shuttlers will be keen to make their mark. Satwik & Chirag, India's top-ranked men's doubles pair, will look to assert their dominance. Lakshya Sen and HS Prannoy in men's singles, along with the promising women's doubles duo of Treesa & Gayatri, and mixed doubles pair Dhruv Kapila & Tanisha Crasto, will all be aiming for deep runs and valuable ranking points. This event is a chance for the entire squad to gain confidence and fine-tune their strategies.

indiaContingent.name: HS Prannoy
indiaContingent.note: Veteran aiming for a deep run
